name: ai_development_os_internal_repo_registry
purpose: Declare the internal and helper repositories AI Development OS can coordinate for prompt-to-product delivery.
canonical_truth: GitHub remote refs, local clean worktrees, repo files, tests, and generated artifacts.

repo_groups.description: Repos or systems that can originate startup/application ideas.
Repos that normalize, route, schedule, and prove prompt-to-product work.
Repos that provide bounded system and code context for agents.
Repos that may contain product implementation, generated source truth, or downstream app work.

repos.allowed_use: Normalize ideas, emit packets, schedule routines, and coordinate proof gates.
Load bounded SRG, documentation graph, workflow, and code-review graph context.

proof_commands: uv run ruff check code_review_graph tests
repos.allowed_use: Export stable source structure for files, modules, symbols, imports, edges, tests, generated artifacts, and risk hints.

repos.branch_rule: main plus codex/* product or contract branches
required_context: Venture Studio idea packet when available
repos.allowed_use: Generate startup/app ideas, export source graph contracts, and host product-boundary truth surfaces.
Hold the actual importer/exporter source readiness product implementation, continuation lanes, VC pitch packet, board go-live packet, and proof artifacts.

repos.branch_rule: create from origin/main or initialize private repo before lanes
repos.allowed_use: Hold the actual application implementation when a startup idea becomes a standalone product.

intake_rules: Treat user or Intelligence Hub idea packets as claims until repo evidence and research/data routes validate them.
Select existing internal repos only when their role matches the product lane.
Create a new private product repo when the idea should not modify an existing product boundary.
Keep Ruflo as coordination memory only; do not use it as repo truth.
Write missing repo access, missing credentials, missing data, or missing expert review as blocker rows with next_valid_move.
